meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Master Claude Code & Antigravity
content.seo_description: Stop wasting hours on bugs. Learn how to leverage Claude Code and Antigravity to debug faster, impress clients, and ship production-ready code with ease.
content.answer_block: To debug effectively, stop guessing and start leveraging AI-driven context analysis. By integrating Claude Code for CLI-based reasoning and Antigravity for real-time state visualization, you transform opaque stack traces into actionable fixes. This workflow cuts debugging time by 70%, allowing you to resolve complex logic errors and maintain high velocity.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and you are staring at a stack trace that makes no sense. You’ve refactored, you’ve console-logged, and you’ve prayed—yet the bug persists. This is the silent killer of freelance velocity: the inability to debug complex, non-deterministic issues within tight delivery windows.

But the era of manual trial-and-error is over. By treating your codebase as a data set for AI to analyze, you stop being a programmer who guesses and start being an architect who resolves. We are going to deploy Claude Code and Antigravity to turn those frustrating late-night debugging sessions into lightning-fast, surgical surgical strikes.

steps.title: Contextualize the Environment
steps.description: Before fixing, you must provide the AI with the complete state. Capture the environment variables and dependencies to ensure the AI understands the constraints of your specific runtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached environment state and identify any configuration mismatches or dependency conflicts that would trigger a runtime error in this production context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude config set context_window 200k && claude scan .

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Use AI to trace the execution path. By providing the stack trace and the code snippet, you force the AI to map the logic flow and pinpoint the exact line of failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Tracing the following stack trace, map the dependency chain and identify the specific function call that deviates from the expected state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the root cause of this stack trace [paste trace]'

steps.title: Generate Hypothesized Fixes
steps.description: Don't just ask for a fix; ask for three distinct approaches: performance-oriented, readability-oriented, and security-hardened. This ensures you choose the best fit for your client's needs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose three distinct refactoring strategies for this bug: one prioritizing speed, one for modularity, and one for error-handling robustness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'provide 3 architectural solutions for this logic bug, comparing trade-offs for each'

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Once the fix is applied, generate an automated unit test to ensure the bug never returns. This builds client trust by demonstrating professional-grade quality assurance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that specifically targets the edge case that caused this bug, ensuring 100% coverage for this logic path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'generate a unit test for the fixed function to prevent regression'

steps.title: Final Verification & Commit
steps.description: Review the AI's final output for readability and merge it into the main branch. Use Claude Code to ensure your commit message is professional and descriptive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the finalized code for stylistic consistency and generate a concise, professional commit message following conventional commits.

pro_tips: Always use the '--verbose' flag in Claude Code to see the chain-of-thought reasoning; it often reveals assumptions the AI is making about your architecture.
Map your project structure in a .claudeignore file to keep the AI focused on the relevant modules and prevent it from hallucinating based on boilerplate code.
When debugging memory leaks, pipe the heap snapshot summary directly into your Antigravity prompt to let the model identify object retention patterns.
Treat the AI as a junior developer: give it the 'why' behind your design patterns so it doesn't suggest refactors that break your established architecture.
Create a 'debugging.md' file in your repo containing common patterns and anti-patterns for your specific tech stack; Claude Code will read this and adapt its suggestions accordingly.
content.next_steps: Now that you have mastered AI-assisted debugging, implement a continuous feedback loop by integrating these tools into your CI/CD pipeline to catch errors before they even reach staging.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. Scale your freelance practice, deliver higher quality work, and reclaim your weekends. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
